purpose: Global catalog for standards families. This file is the first thing an agent should open when it needs to understand how standards are grouped, named, and surfaced by the kernel.

naming_contract_shape.principle: single sentence naming doctrine for the whole standards tree
naming_contract_shape.rules: ordered list of durable naming rules

folder_pattern_shape.markdown_guide_file: optional human companion pattern such as std_<artifact>.md
folder_pattern_shape.generated_projection_file: string pattern for mechanically regenerated projections such as <artifact>_index.json
folder_pattern_shape.note: short explanation of how group roots and legacy root-level standards coexist

group_shape.group_id: stable lowercase token used in file names, kernel displays, and future selectors
group_shape.title: human-readable family title
group_shape.group_root: repo-relative directory that owns the family
group_shape.authority_index: repo-relative path to the family's authority index JSON
group_shape.summary: one-line explanation of what belongs in the family
group_shape.kernel_navigation_hints: ordered list of short hints for file-tree or kernel-first navigation

validation_rules: Every group entry must point to a real authority_index JSON file.
Group ids should stay stable even if titles evolve.
The registry should describe where to start, not duplicate every per-artifact rule from the group indexes.
Names must optimize for grep, file-tree browsing, and agent recovery, not brevity.

purpose.purpose: Compact browse row for selecting a standard by artifact kind, family, and authority posture.

contract.purpose: Working card for the standard's required fields, authority split, companion status, and safe mutation lane.
forbidden: treating a registry row as the standard body

schema.purpose: Schema and field-level drilldown for authoring or repairing one governed artifact kind.

validators.purpose: Validation and compliance drilldown for proving the standard and its generated surfaces still match.
minimum_payload: builder command when generated projections exist
forbidden: claiming compliance without a scanner or explicit no-scanner receipt
refreshing generated outputs when source-coupling is blocked

navigable_scopes.rule: One std_* contract file or standard module.
One markdown companion or human-readable guide tied to a standard.
One standards registry or authority-index row that locates a standard.
One validator, builder, scanner adapter, or checker command that proves the standard.

navigable_facets.rule: Expose required artifact fields before mutation.
Separate authored source, generated projection, registry row, and compliance evidence.
Name the command or adapter that validates the standard.
Expose companion markdown status without treating companion prose as source authority.

currentness_policy.rule: Standards rows are source-authored; generated standards catalog, standard-skill map, compliance ledger, and agent bootstrap projections must be refreshed or checked by their owners before generated output is committed.

dependency_neighborhood_policy.rule: Expose the group authority index, sibling standards, companion docs, scanner adapter, authoring skill, and generated projections as first-order edges; downshift wider standards groups to counts and cluster drilldowns.
